title: जगन्नाथ सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री जगन्नाथ सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् (मुख्य श्लोक) ॥

ॐ जगन्नाथो जगद्वन्द्यो जगदीशो जगत्पतिः।
जगत्पिता जगन्मातृ जगत्साक्षी जगद्गुरुः॥ १॥

नीलाचलनिवासी च नीलमाधवसंज्ञकः।
पुरुषोत्तमक्षेत्रेशो रत्नसिंहासनस्थितः॥ २॥

सुभद्रासहितो देवो बलभद्रसमन्वितः।
सुदर्शनधरो विष्णुर्नारायणसनातनः॥ ३॥

पतितपावनो देवो महाप्रसादप्रदायकः।
गुण्डिचायात्रकर्ता च रथयात्राविहारकृत्॥ ४॥

दारुब्रह्मस्वरूपश्च अनादिरनन्तकः।
कृष्णो गोपालो गोविन्दो वासुदेवश्च माधवः॥ ५॥

श्रीधरो हृषीकेशश्च पद्मनाभो जनार्दनः।
त्रिविक्रमो वामनश्च कल्किर्दशावतारकः॥ ६॥

सर्वभूतहृदावासो भक्तवत्सलमण्डनः।
मुक्तिदो भुक्तिदश्चैव सर्वकामप्रदायकः॥ ७॥

सहस्रनामभिर्दिव्यैर्जगन्नाथमुपास्महे।
भुक्तिमुक्तिप्रदातारं नीलाचलनिवासिनम्॥ ८॥

॥ इति श्री जगन्नाथ सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम् (मुख्य अंश) सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Jagannath Sahasranama Stotram (Mukhya Shloka) ||

Om Jagannatho Jagadvandyo Jagadisho Jagatpatih |
Jagatpita Jaganmatri Jagatsakshi Jagadguruh || 1 ||

Nilachala Nivasi Cha Nilamadhava Samjnakah |
Purushottama Kshetresho Ratna Simhasana Sthitah || 2 ||

Subhadra Sahito Devo Balabhadra Samanvitah |
Sudarshanadharo Vishnur Narayana Sanatanah || 3 ||

Patitapavano Devo Mahaprasada Pradayakah |
Gundicha Yatra Karta Cha Rathayatra Viharakrit || 4 ||

Darubrahma Svarupash Cha Anadir Anantakah |
Krishno Gopalo Govindo Vasudevash Cha Madhavah || 5 ||

Shridharo Hrishikeshash Cha Padmanabho Janardanah |
Trivikramo Vamanash Cha Kalkir Dashavatarak || 6 ||

Sarvabhuta Hridavaso Bhaktavatsala Mandanah |
Muktido Bhuktidash Chaiva Sarvakama Pradayakah || 7 ||

Sahasranamabhir Divyair Jagannatham Upasmahe |
Bhukti Mukti Pradataram Nilachala Nivasinam || 8 ||

|| Iti Shri Jagannath Sahasranama Stotram (Mukhya Amsha) Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
जगन्नाथ, जगद्वन्द्य, जगदीश, जगत्पति, जगत्पिता, जगन्माता, जगत्साक्षी, जगद्गुरु।

नीलाचलनिवासी, नीलमाधव, पुरुषोत्तम क्षेत्र के ईश, रत्नसिंहासन पर स्थित।

सुभद्रा सहित, बलभद्र समन्वित, सुदर्शनधर, विष्णु, नारायण, सनातन।

पतितपावन, महाप्रसादप्रदायक, गुण्डिचा यात्रा करने वाले, रथयात्रा विहारकृत।

दारुब्रह्मस्वरूप (लकड़ी में ब्रह्म), अनादि, अनन्त, कृष्ण, गोपाल, गोविन्द।

दिव्य सहस्रनामों से जगन्नाथ की उपासना करें — वे भुक्ति-मुक्ति प्रदान करें।
benefits: Thousand names of Lord Jagannath of Puri Dham
Unique names including Darubrahmma, Nilamadhava, Mahaprasadpradayaka
Invokes Jagannath with Subhadra and Balabhadra for complete devotion
Invokes both material enjoyment (bhukti) and liberation (mukti)
Ideal for Rath Yatra, Snana Yatra and Jagannath temple worship
description: Jagannath Sahasranama Stotram (जगन्नाथ सहस्रनाम स्तोत्रम्) is a sacred recitation of 1000 divine names dedicated to Lord Jagannath. Drawn from Skanda Purana / Oriya Tradition, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to invoke Jagannath with Subhadra and Balabhadra for complete devotion, grant both material enjoyment (bhukti) and liberation (mukti), and unique names including Darubrahmma, Nilamadhava, Mahaprasadpradayaka. Recitation is particularly auspicious on Thursday (Guruvar) and Saturday (Shanivar) and during Rath Yatra. At 8 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 6 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Jagannath Sahasranama Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Jagannath Sahasranama in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Thousand names of Lord Jagannath of Puri for bhukti and mukti.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Jagannath Sahasranama Stotram and what does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Jagannath Sahasranama Stotram is a litany of one thousand sacred names (sahasra = thousand, nama = name) of Lord Jagannath, the presiding deity of the Puri temple in Odisha and one of the Char Dham pilgrimage sites. The hymn enumerates names invoking Jagannath as Purushottama (the supreme person), Nilamadhava (the dark-blue Krishna of Nilachala), Daru-Brahma (the wood-form of the Absolute), Patitapavana (purifier of the fallen), Jagatpati (Lord of the universe) and as identical with all the Dashavataras of Vishnu. It is preserved in classical Vaishnava tradition and recited by devotees of the Puri Jagannath cult. (Sources: Puri Jagannath temple liturgy; Hindupedia Jagannath; Skanda Purana Purushottama Mahatmya.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Jagannath and how is he related to Krishna and Vishnu?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Jagannath (literally Lord of the Universe) is worshipped at Puri in Odisha as a swayambhu form of Vishnu-Krishna, distinctively carved from neem wood (daru-brahma) with large round eyes, stub-arms and no legs, accompanied by his elder brother Balabhadra and sister Subhadra. Vaishnava tradition identifies him with Krishna himself transferred from Dwarka, while Shakta and Buddhist streams have absorbed him as Bhairava and as the Buddha. The Skanda Purana Purushottama Mahatmya recounts how King Indradyumna brought the deity to Nilachala after the Sabar chieftain Vishvavasu's secret worship of Nilamadhava. Adi Shankara established Puri as the eastern Char Dham. (Sources: Skanda Purana Utkala Khanda; Hindupedia Jagannath; Puri temple history.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Puri Rath Yatra in Jagannath worship?
seoFaqs.answer: The Rath Yatra (chariot festival) held on Ashadha Shukla Dwitiya (June-July) is the most famous Jagannath festival, when the deities Jagannath, Balabhadra and Subhadra are placed on three giant wooden chariots (Nandighosha, Taladhwaja and Darpadalana) and pulled by lakhs of devotees along the Bada Danda from the Puri temple to the Gundicha temple, where they stay for nine days before the Bahuda Yatra return. The English word juggernaut derives from this awe-inspiring procession. Devotees often recite the Jagannath Sahasranama during the Rath Yatra week, regarding even the sight of the chariots as moksha-giving. (Sources: Skanda Purana; Drikpanchang Rath Yatra; Puri temple Niti calendar.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Jagannath Sahasranama be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: Sacred occasions for recitation include the entire Ashadha month culminating in Rath Yatra, Snana Yatra (Jyeshtha Purnima, the bathing festival), Anavasara fortnight, Navakalevara (the once-in-twelve-or-nineteen-years body-renewal ceremony), Krishna Janmashtami, every Ekadashi, the Kartik month, and any pilgrimage to Puri. Daily recitation in brahma-muhurta (4-6 AM) facing east, or before a Jagannath image, is the Gaudiya Vaishnava tradition. Bengali and Odia devotees particularly recite it on Saturdays and during the Chaturmasya when Jagannath is said to rest at Puri. (Sources: Puri temple Niti calendar; Drikpanchang; Gaudiya Vaishnava sadhana literature.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the story of Daru Brahma and the wooden form of Jagannath?
seoFaqs.answer: Unique among major Hindu deities, Jagannath, Balabhadra and Subhadra are carved from sacred neem-wood logs called daru-brahma rather than stone or metal, and the idols are ritually replaced every twelve or nineteen years in the Navakalevara ceremony. The Skanda Purana relates that King Indradyumna, unable to find Nilamadhava after the Sabar Vishvavasu's worship was disrupted, was instructed in a dream to fashion the deity from a fragrant log washing ashore. Vishvakarma (or Vishnu himself in disguise) carved the figures behind closed doors but left them unfinished with stub-arms when the king broke the silence — an iconography theologians read as the Absolute being formally incomplete since it transcends form. (Sources: Skanda Purana Utkala Khanda; Hindupedia Daru Brahma; Puri Navakalevara records.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Jagannath worship relate to Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu and Gaudiya Vaishnavism?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Chaitanya Mahaprabhu (1486-1534), the founder of Gaudiya Vaishnavism and originator of the modern sankirtana movement, spent the last twenty-four years of his life at Puri in a hut called Gambhira beside the Jagannath temple, regarding Jagannath as none other than Krishna himself in viraha-rasa (the mood of separation from Radha). His ecstatic dance before the Rath Yatra chariots, his Sikshashtakam composed there, and his disappearance into the Tota Gopinath temple have made Puri the spiritual capital of Gaudiya Vaishnavas and ISKCON. The Jagannath Sahasranama is recited daily by devotees in this lineage as part of their nama-japa. (Sources: Chaitanya Charitamrita Krishnadasa Kaviraja; Hindupedia Gaudiya Vaishnavism; ISKCON Puri.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Jagannath Sahasranama promise to its reciters?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition holds that reciting the thousand names of Jagannath grants liberation (mukti), the four purusharthas of dharma-artha-kama-moksha, freedom from rebirth in lower wombs, protection during travel and pilgrimage, healing from chronic illnesses (Jagannath being known as Patitapavana, the purifier of the fallen), and especially the rare boon of mahaprasada-sevana — eligibility to receive the temple's sacred offering that is said to never become impure. The Skanda Purana Utkala Khanda specifically promises that those who chant Jagannath's names attain Vaikuntha and serve the Lord eternally at Nilachala. The recitation is regarded as particularly potent for healing skin diseases, blindness and lifelong karmic obstructions. (Sources: Skanda Purana Utkala Khanda phalashruti; Hindupedia Jagannath; Puri temple tradition.)
